figures.caption: Table 6: Cost accounting protocol used in our comparisons.
Figure 1: Preparation-free heterogeneous cooperative perception. (a) Prior works; (b) BOLT.
Figure 8: Additional qualitative BEV results for SECOND→LSS-E on DAIR-V2X (top) and OPV2V (bottom). Each panel shows the full BEV view together with a zoomed ROI from the highlighted region.
Figure 7: Additional qualitative BEV results for SECOND→PP on DAIR-V2X (top) and OPV2V (bottom). Each panel shows the full BEV view together with a zoomed ROI from the highlighted region.

Table 2: Preparation-free cooperative perception on DAIR-V2X and OPV2V. PP and SECOND are LiDAR encoders; LSS-E and LSS-R50 are Lift-Splat camera encoders with EfficientNet and ResNet-50 backbones. w/o Plugin uses independently trained single-agent branches with a frozen PyramidFusion cooperative stack and no online adaptation. w/ Plugin enables BOLT under the same setting. Each result uses one ego and one active collaborator. Green cells surpass ego-only; ∆ is the AP@50 gain from the plugin.
Figure 4: Qualitative BEV results. Top: DAIR-V2X; bottom: OPV2V. Left: w/o plugin; right: w/ plugin (BOLT).

Table 1: Comparison of heterogeneous cooperative perception methods under deployment-relevant properties. Preparation-Free = a target collaborator can be deployed without any cooperative preparation (offline cooperative training, collaborator-specific support set, etc.). Interface Handling summarizes where or how cross-modal incompatibility is resolved.

Figure 3: Framework overview. The ego agent’s frozen single-agent path (top) produces teacher predictions. The neighbor agent transmits its BEV feature to the ego side, where the received feature passes through the adaptive plugin Pθ (AdaIN → residual adapter → per-channel gate) before entering the frozen fusion module. Only Pθ is updated online via ego-as-teacher distillation; all other components remain frozen.

Figure 9: Dynamic online convergence of BOLT on DAIR-V2X (PP→SECOND). AP@50 is computed on the prefix processed so far and plotted every 10 samples for BOLT, a standalone PP ego-only detector, and no-plugin under the same stream order. BOLT quickly recovers from degraded preparation-free cooperation, overtakes the standalone ego-only baseline, and remains stable thereafter.
Figure 5: Additional qualitative BEV results for PP→LSS-E on DAIR-V2X (top) and OPV2V (bottom). Each panel shows the full BEV view together with a zoomed ROI from the highlighted region.
Figure 6: Additional qualitative BEV results for PP→LSS-R50 on DAIR-V2X (top) and OPV2V (bottom). Each panel shows the full BEV view together with a zoomed ROI from the highlighted region.
Table 4: Ablation studies on DAIR-V2X (PP→LSS-E). Default configuration highlighted in gray.

Figure 10: Precision–recall curves at IoU 0.5 on DAIR-V2X (PP→LSS-E). Adding the enhancement loss (τhi=0.3, boost=0.1) improves the high-confidence part of the curve and slightly increases AP@50, while the low-precision tail remains close to the no-boost baseline.

Table 7: Approximate wall-clock cost references on DAIR-V2X (PP→SECOND) using one NVIDIA RTX 4090. Times are rough implementation-level estimates intended for scale comparison only, not a strict benchmark.
